book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
The Fifth Theological Oration. On the Holy Spirit.

ccel.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

title: The Fifth Theological Oration. On the Holy Spirit.

source.title: NPNF2-07. Cyril of Jerusalem, Gregory Nazianzen

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Fifth Theological Oration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such then is the account of the Son, and in this manner He has escaped those who would stone Him, passing through the midst of them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the Word is not stoned, but casts stones when He pleases; and uses a sling against wild beasts—that is, words—approaching the Mount in an unholy way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, they go on, what have you to say about the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: From whence are you bringing in upon us this strange God, of Whom Scripture is silent?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And even they who keep within bounds as to the Son speak thus.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And just as we find in the case of roads and rivers, that they split off from one another and join again, so it happens also in this case, through the superabundance of impiety, that people who differ in all other respects have here some points of agreement, so that you never can tell for certain either where they are of one mind, or where they are in conflict.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now the subject of the Holy Spirit presents a special difficulty, not only because when these men have become weary in their disputations concerning the Son, they struggle with greater heat against the Spirit (for it seems to be absolutely necessary for them to have some object on which to give expression to their impiety, or life would appear to them no longer worth living), but further because we ourselves also, being worn out by the multitude of their questions, are in something of the same condition with men who have lost their appetite; who having taken a dislike to some particular kind of food, shrink from all food; so we in like manner have an aversion from all discussions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yet may the Spirit grant it to us, and then the discourse will proceed, and God will be glorified.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well then, we will leave to others who have worked upon this subject for us as well as for themselves, as we have worked upon it for them, the task of examining carefully and distinguishing in how many senses the word Spirit or the word Holy is used and understood in Holy Scripture, with the evidence suitable to such an enquiry; and of shewing how besides these the combination of the two words—I mean, Holy Spirit—is used in a peculiar sense; but we will apply ourselves to the remainder of the subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: They then who are angry with us on the ground that we are bringing in a strange or interpolated God, viz.:—the Holy Ghost, and who fight so very hard for the letter, should know that they are afraid where no fear is; and I would have them clearly understand that their love for the letter is but a cloak for their impiety, as shall be shewn later on, when we refute their objections to the utmost of our power.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But we have so much confidence in the Deity of the Spirit Whom we adore, that we will begin our teaching concerning His Godhead by fitting to Him the Names which belong to the Trinity, even though some persons may think us too bold.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Father was the True Light which lighteneth every man coming into the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Son was the True Light which lighteneth every man coming into the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Other Comforter was the True Light which lighteneth every man coming into the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Was and Was and Was, but Was One Thing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Light thrice repeated; but One Light and One God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This was what David represented to himself long before when he said, In Thy Light shall we see Light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And now we have both seen and proclaim concisely and simply the doctrine of God the Trinity, comprehending out of Light (the Father), Light (the Son), in Light (the Holy Ghost).

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He that rejects it, let him reject it; and he that doeth iniquity, let him do iniquity; we proclaim that which we have understood.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We will get us up into a high mountain, and will shout, if we be not heard, below; we will exalt the Spirit; we will not be afraid; or if we are afraid, it shall be of keeping silence, not of proclaiming.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If ever there was a time when the Father was not, then there was a time when the Son was not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If ever there was a time when the Son was not, then there was a time when the Spirit was not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If the One was from the beginning, then the Three were so too.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If you throw down the One, I am bold to assert that you do not set up the other Two.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what profit is there in an imperfect Godhead?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or rather, what Godhead can there be if It is not perfect?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And how can that be perfect which lacks something of perfection?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And surely there is something lacking if it hath not the Holy, and how would it have this if it were without the Spirit?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For either holiness is something different from Him, and if so let some one tell me what it is conceived to be; or if it is the same, how is it not from the beginning, as if it were better for God to be at one time imperfect and apart from the Spirit?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If He is not from the beginning, He is in the same rank with myself, even though a little before me; for we are both parted from Godhead by time.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If He is in the same rank with myself, how can He make me God, or join me with Godhead?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or rather, let me reason with you about Him from a somewhat earlier point, for we have already discussed the Trinity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Sadducees altogether denied the existence of the Holy Spirit, just as they did that of Angels and the Resurrection; rejecting, I know not upon what ground, the important testimonies concerning Him in the Old Testament.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And of the Greeks those who are more inclined to speak of God, and who approach nearest to us, have formed some conception of Him, as it seems to me, though they have differed as to His Name, and have addressed Him as the Mind of the World, or the External Mind, and the like.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But of the wise men amongst ourselves, some have conceived of him as an Activity, some as a Creature, some as God; and some have been uncertain which to call Him, out of reverence for Scripture, they say, as though it did not make the matter clear either way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And therefore they neither worship Him nor treat Him with dishonour, but take up a neutral position, or rather a very miserable one, with respect to Him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And of those who consider Him to be God, some are orthodox in mind only, while others venture to be so with the lips also.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And I have heard of some who are even more clever, and measure Deity; and these agree with us that there are Three Conceptions; but they have separated these from one another so completely as to make one of them infinite both in essence and power, and the second in power but not in essence, and the third circumscribed in both; thus imitating in another way those who call them the Creator, the Co-operator, and the Minister, and consider that the same order and dignity which belongs to these names is also a sequence in the facts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But we cannot enter into any discussion with those who do not even believe in His existence, nor with the Greek babblers (for we would not be enriched in our argument with the oil of sinners).

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: With the others, however, we will argue thus.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Holy Ghost must certainly be conceived of either as in the category of the Self-existent, or as in that of the things which are contemplated in another; of which classes those who are skilled in such matters call the one Substance and the other Accident.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now if He were an Accident, He would be an Activity of God, for what else, or of whom else, could He be, for surely this is what most avoids composition?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if He is an Activity, He will be effected, but will not effect and will cease to exist as soon as He has been effected, for this is the nature of an Activity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: How is it then that He acts and says such and such things, and defines, and is grieved, and is angered, and has all the qualities which belong clearly to one that moves, and not to movement?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if He is a Substance and not an attribute of Substance, He will be conceived of either as a Creature of God, or as God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For anything between these two, whether having nothing in common with either, or a compound of both, not even they who invented the goat-stag could imagine.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now, if He is a creature, how do we believe in Him, how are we made perfect in Him?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is not the same thing to believe IN a thing and to believe About it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The one belongs to Deity, the other to—any thing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if He is God, then He is neither a creature, nor a thing made, nor a fellow servant, nor any of these lowly appellations.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There—the word is with you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let the slings be let go; let the syllogism be woven.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Either He is altogether Unbegotten, or else He is Begotten.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If He is Unbegotten, there are two Unoriginates.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If he is Begotten, you must make a further subdivision.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is so either by the Father or by the Son.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if by the Father, there are two Sons, and they are Brothers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And you may make them twins if you like, or the one older and the other younger, since you are so very fond of the bodily conceptions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if by the Son, then such a one will say, we get a glimpse of a Grandson God, than which nothing could be more absurd.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For my part however, if I saw the necessity of the distinction, I should have acknowledged the facts without fear of the names.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it does not follow that because the Son is the Son in some higher relation (inasmuch as we could not in any other way than this point out that He is of God and Consubstantial), it would also be necessary to think that all the names of this lower world and of our kindred should be transferred to the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or may be you would consider our God to be a male, according to the same arguments, because he is called God and Father, and that Deity is feminine, from the gender of the word, and Spirit neuter, because It has nothing to do with generation; But if you would be silly enough to say, with the old myths and fables, that God begat the Son by a marriage with His own Will, we should be introduced to the Hermaphrodite god of Marcion and Valentinus who imagined these newfangled Æons.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since we do not admit your first division, which declares that there is no mean between Begotten and Unbegotten, at once, along with your magnificent division, away go your Brothers and your Grandsons, as when the first link of an intricate chain is broken they are broken with it, and disappear from your system of divinity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For, tell me, what position will you assign to that which Proceeds, which has started up between the two terms of your division, and is introduced by a better Theologian than you, our Saviour Himself?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or perhaps you have taken that word out of your Gospels for the sake of your Third Testament, The Holy Ghost, which proceedeth from the Father; Who, inasmuch as He proceedeth from That Source, is no Creature; and inasmuch as He is not Begotten is no Son; and inasmuch as He is between the Unbegotten and the Begotten is God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And thus escaping the toils of your syllogisms, He has manifested himself as God, stronger than your divisions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What then is Procession?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you tell me what is the Unbegottenness of the Father, and I will explain to you the physiology of the Generation of the Son and the Procession of the Spirit, and we shall both of us be frenzy-stricken for prying into the mystery of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And who are we to do these things, we who cannot even see what lies at our feet, or number the sand of the sea, or the drops of rain, or the days of Eternity, much less enter into the Depths of God, and supply an account of that Nature which is so unspeakable and transcending all words?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What then, say they, is there lacking to the Spirit which prevents His being a Son, for if there were not something lacking He would be a Son?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We assert that there is nothing lacking—for God has no deficiency.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But the difference of manifestation, if I may so express myself, or rather of their mutual relations one to another, has caused the difference of their Names.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For indeed it is not some deficiency in the Son which prevents His being Father (for Sonship is not a deficiency), and yet He is not Father.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: According to this line of argument there must be some deficiency in the Father, in respect of His not being Son.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the Father is not Son, and yet this is not due to either deficiency or subjection of Essence; but the very fact of being Unbegotten or Begotten, or Proceeding has given the name of Father to the First, of the Son to the Second, and of the Third, Him of Whom we are speaking, of the Holy Ghost that the distinction of the Three Persons may be preserved in the one nature and dignity of the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For neither is the Son Father, for the Father is One, but He is what the Father is; nor is the Spirit Son because He is of God, for the Only-begotten is One, but He is what the Son is.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Three are One in Godhead, and the One Three in properties; so that neither is the Unity a Sabellian one, nor does the Trinity countenance the present evil distinction.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is the Spirit God?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Most certainly.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well then, is He Consubstantial?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yes, if He is God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Grant me, says my opponent, that there spring from the same Source One who is a Son, and One who is not a Son, and these of One Substance with the Source, and I admit a God and a God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nay, if you will grant me that there is another God and another nature of God I will give you the same Trinity with the same name and facts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since God is One and the Supreme Nature is One, how can I present to you the Likeness?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or will you seek it again in lower regions and in your own surroundings?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is very shameful, and not only shameful, but very foolish, to take from things below a guess at things above, and from a fluctuating nature at the things that are unchanging, and as Isaiah says, to seek the Living among the dead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But yet I will try, for your sake, to give you some assistance for your argument, even from that source.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I think I will pass over other points, though I might bring forward many from animal history, some generally known, others only known to a few, of what nature has contrived with wonderful art in connection with the generation of animals.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For not only are likes said to beget likes, and things diverse to beget things diverse, but also likes to be begotten by things diverse, and things diverse by likes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if we may believe the story, there is yet another mode of generation, when an animal is self-consumed and self-begotten.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There are also creatures which depart in some sort from their true natures, and undergo change and transformation from one creature into another, by a magnificence of nature.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And indeed sometimes in the same species part may be generated and part not; and yet all of one substance; which is more like our present subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will just mention one fact of our own nature which every one knows, and then I will pass on to another part of the subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What was Adam?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A creature of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What then was Eve?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A fragment of the creature.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what was Seth?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The begotten of both.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Does it then seem to you that Creature and Fragment and Begotten are the same thing?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of course it does not.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But were not these persons consubstantial?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of course they were.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well then, here it is an acknowledged fact that different persons may have the same substance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I say this, not that I would attribute creation or fraction or any property of body to the Godhead (let none of your contenders for a word be down upon me again), but that I may contemplate in these, as on a stage, things which are objects of thought alone.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is not possible to trace out any image exactly to the whole extent of the truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, they say, what is the meaning of all this?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For is not the one an offspring, and the other a something else of the One?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Did not both Eve and Seth come from the one Adam?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And were they both begotten by him?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: No; but the one was a fragment of him, and the other was begotten by him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And yet the two were one and the same thing; both were human beings; no one will deny that.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Will you then give up your contention against the Spirit, that He must be either altogether begotten, or else cannot be consubstantial, or be God; and admit from human examples the possibility of our position?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I think it will be well for you, unless you are determined to be very quarrelsome, and to fight against what is proved to demonstration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But, he says, who in ancient or modern times ever worshipped the Spirit?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Who ever prayed to Him?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Where is it written that we ought to worship Him, or to pray to Him, and whence have you derived this tenet of yours?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We will give the more perfect reason hereafter, when we discuss the question of the unwritten; for the present it will suffice to say that it is the Spirit in Whom we worship, and in Whom we pray.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For Scripture says, God is a Spirit, and they that worship Him must worship Him in Spirit and in truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And again,—We know not what we should pray for as we ought; but the Spirit Itself maketh intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered; and I will pray with the Spirit and I will pray with the understanding also;—that is, in the mind and in the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore to adore or to pray to the Spirit seems to me to be simply Himself offering prayer or adoration to Himself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what godly or learned man would disapprove of this, because in fact the adoration of One is the adoration of the Three, because of the equality of honour and Deity between the Three?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So I will not be frightened by the argument that all things are said to have been made by the Son; as if the Holy Spirit also were one of these things.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it says all things that were made, and not simply all things.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the Father was not, nor were any of the things that were not made.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Prove that He was made, and then give Him to the Son, and number Him among the creatures; but until you can prove this you will gain nothing for your impiety from this comprehensive phrase.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if He was made, it was certainly through Christ; I myself would not deny that.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if He was not made, how can He be either one of the All, or through Christ?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Cease then to dishonour the Father in your opposition to the Only-begotten (for it is no real honour, by presenting to Him a creature to rob Him of what is more valuable, a Son), and to dishonour the Son in your opposition to the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For He is not the Maker of a Fellow servant, but He is glorified with One of co-equal honour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Rank no part of the Trinity with thyself, lest thou fall away from the Trinity; cut not off from Either the One and equally august Nature; because if thou overthrow any of the Three thou wilt have overthrown the whole.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Better to take a meagre view of the Unity than to venture on a complete impiety.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Our argument has now come to its principal point; and I am grieved that a problem that was long dead, and that had given way to faith, is now stirred up afresh; yet it is necessary to stand against these praters, and not to let judgment go by default, when we have the Word on our side, and are pleading the cause of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If, say they, there is God and God and God, how is it that there are not Three Gods, or how is it that what is glorified is not a plurality of Principles?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Who is it who say this?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Those who have reached a more complete ungodliness, or even those who have taken the secondary part; I mean who are moderate in a sense in respect of the Son.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For my argument is partly against both in common, partly against these latter in particular.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What I have to say in answer to these is as follows:—What right have you who worship the Son, even though you have revolted from the Spirit, to call us Tritheists?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Are not you Ditheists?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if you deny also the worship of the Only Begotten, you have clearly ranged yourself among our adversaries.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And why should we deal kindly with you as not quite dead?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if you do worship Him, and are so far in the way of salvation, we will ask you what reasons you have to give for your ditheism, if you are charged with it?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If there is in you a word of wisdom answer, and open to us also a way to an answer.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the very same reason with which you will repel a charge of Ditheism will prove sufficient for us against one of Tritheism.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And thus we shall win the day by making use of you our accusers as our Advocates, than which nothing can be more generous.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What is our quarrel and dispute with both?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To us there is One God, for the Godhead is One, and all that proceedeth from Him is referred to One, though we believe in Three Persons.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For one is not more and another less God; nor is One before and another after; nor are They divided in will or parted in power; nor can you find here any of the qualities of divisible things; but the Godhead is, to speak concisely, undivided in separate Persons; and there is one mingling of Light, as it were of three suns joined to each other.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When then we look at the Godhead, or the First Cause, or the Monarchia, that which we conceive is One; but when we look at the Persons in Whom the Godhead dwells, and at Those Who timelessly and with equal glory have their Being from the First Cause—there are Three Whom we worship.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What of that, they will say perhaps; do not the Greeks also believe in one Godhead, as their more advanced philosophers declare?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And with us Humanity is one, namely the entire race; but yet they have many gods, not One, just as there are many men.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But in this case the common nature has a unity which is only conceivable in thought; and the individuals are parted from one another very far indeed, both by time and by dispositions and by power.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we are not only compound beings, but also contrasted beings, both with one another and with ourselves; nor do we remain entirely the same for a single day, to say nothing of a whole lifetime, but both in body and in soul are in a perpetual state of flow and change.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And perhaps the same may be said of the Angels and the whole of that superior nature which is second to the Trinity alone; although they are simple in some measure and more fixed in good, owing to their nearness to the highest Good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nor do those whom the Greeks worship as gods, and (to use their own expression) dæmons, need us in any respect for their accusers, but are convicted upon the testimony of their own theologians, some as subject to passion, some as given to faction, and full of innumerable evils and changes, and in a state of opposition, not only to one another, but even to their first causes, whom they call Oceani and Tethyes and Phanetes, and by several other names; and last of all a certain god who hated his children through his lust of rule, and swallowed up all the rest through his greediness that he might become the father of all men and gods whom he miserably devoured, and then vomited forth again.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if these are but myths and fables, as they say in order to escape the shamefulness of the story, what will they say in reference to the dictum that all things are divided into three parts, and that each god presides over a different part of the Universe, having a distinct province as well as a distinct rank?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But our faith is not like this, nor is this the portion of Jacob, says my Theologian.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But each of these Persons possesses Unity, not less with that which is United to it than with itself, by reason of the identity of Essence and Power.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And this is the account of the Unity, so far as we have apprehended it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If then this account is the true one, let us thank God for the glimpse He has granted us; if it is not let us seek for a better.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As for the arguments with which you would overthrow the Union which we support, I know not whether we should say you are jesting or in earnest.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what is this argument?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “Things of one essence, you say, are counted together,” and by this “counted together,” you mean that they are collected into one number.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But things which are not of one essence are not thus counted…so that you cannot avoid speaking of three gods, according to this account, while we do not run any risk at all of it, inasmuch as we assert that they are not consubstantial.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so by a single word you have freed yourselves from trouble, and have gained a pernicious victory, for in fact you have done something like what men do when they hang themselves for fear of death.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For to save yourselves trouble in your championship of the Monarchia you have denied the Godhead, and abandoned the question to your opponents.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But for my part, even if labor should be necessary, I will not abandon the Object of my adoration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And yet on this point I cannot see where the difficulty is.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You say, Things of one essence are counted together, but those which are not consubstantial are reckoned one by one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Where did you get this from?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: From what teachers of dogma or mythology?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you not know that every number expresses the quantity of what is included under it, and not the nature of the things?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But I am so old fashioned, or perhaps I should say so unlearned, as to use the word Three of that number of things, even if they are of a different nature, and to use One and One and One in a different way of so many units, even if they are united in essence, looking not so much at the things themselves as at the quantity of the things in respect of which the enumeration is made.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since you hold so very close to the letter (although you are contending against the letter), pray take your demonstrations from this source.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There are in the Book of Proverbs three things which go well, a lion, a goat, and a cock; and to these is added a fourth;—a King making a speech before the people, to pass over the other sets of four which are there counted up, although things of various natures.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And I find in Moses two Cherubim counted singly.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But now, in your technology, could either the former things be called three, when they differ so greatly in their nature, or the latter be treated as units when they are so closely connected and of one nature?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if I were to speak of God and Mammon, as two masters, reckoned under one head, when they are so very different from each other, I should probably be still more laughed at for such a connumeration.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But to my mind, he says, those things are said to be connumerated and of the same essence of which the names also correspond, as Three Men, or Three gods, but not Three this and that.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What does this concession amount to?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It is suitable to one laying down the law as to names, not to one who is asserting the truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I also will assert that Peter and James and John are not three or consubstantial, so long as I cannot say Three Peters, or Three Jameses, or Three Johns; for what you have reserved for common names we demand also for proper names, in accordance with your arrangement; or else you will be unfair in not conceding to others what you assume for yourself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What about John then, when in his Catholic Epistle he says that there are Three that bear witness, the Spirit and the Water and the Blood?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you think he is talking nonsense?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: First, because he has ventured to reckon under one numeral things which are not consubstantial, though you say this ought to be done only in the case of things which are consubstantial.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For who would assert that these are consubstantial?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Secondly, because he has not been consistent in the way he has happened upon his terms; for after using Three in the masculine gender he adds three words which are neuter, contrary to the definitions and laws which you and your grammarians have laid down.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what is the difference between putting a masculine Three first, and then adding One and One and One in the neuter, or after a masculine One and One and One to use the Three not in the masculine but in the neuter, which you yourself disclaim in the case of Deity?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What have you to say about the Crab, which may mean either an animal, or an instrument, or a constellation?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And what about the Dog, now terrestrial, now aquatic, now celestial?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you not see that three crabs or dogs are spoken of?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why of course it is so.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Well then, are they therefore of one substance?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: None but a fool would say that.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So you see how completely your argument from connumeration has broken down, and is refuted by all these instances.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if things that are of one substance are not always counted under one numeral, and things not of one substance are thus counted, and the pronunciation of the name once for all is used in both cases, what advantage do you gain towards your doctrine?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will look also at this further point, which is not without its bearing on the subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: One and One added together make Two; and Two resolved again becomes One and One, as is perfectly evident.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If, however, elements which are added together must, as your theory requires, be consubstantial, and those which are separate be heterogeneous, then it will follow that the same things must be both consubstantial and heterogeneous.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: No: I laugh at your Counting Before and your Counting After, of which you are so proud, as if the facts themselves depended upon the order of their names.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If this were so, according to the same law, since the same things are in consequence of the equality of their nature counted in Holy Scripture, sometimes in an earlier, sometimes in a later place, what prevents them from being at once more honourable and less honourable than themselves?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I say the same of the names God and Lord, and of the prepositions Of Whom, and By Whom, and In Whom, by which you describe the Deity according to the rules of art for us, attributing the first to the Father, the second to the Son, and the third to the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what would you have done, if each of these expressions were constantly allotted to Each Person, when, the fact being that they are used of all the Persons, as is evident to those who have studied the question, you even so make them the ground of such inequality both of nature and dignity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is sufficient for all who are not altogether wanting in sense.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since it is a matter of difficulty for you after you have once made an assault upon the Spirit, to check your rush, and not rather like a furious boar to push your quarrel to the bitter end, and to thrust yourself upon the knife until you have received the whole wound in your own breast; let us go on to see what further argument remains to you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Over and over again you turn upon us the silence of Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But that it is not a strange doctrine, nor an afterthought, but acknowledged and plainly set forth both by the ancients and many of our own day, is already demonstrated by many persons who have treated of this subject, and who have handled the Holy Scriptures, not with indifference or as a mere pastime, but have gone beneath the letter and looked into the inner meaning, and have been deemed worthy to see the hidden beauty, and have been irradiated by the light of knowledge.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: We, however in our turn will briefly prove it as far as may be, in order not to seem to be over-curious or improperly ambitious, building on another’s foundation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But since the fact, that Scripture does not very clearly or very often write Him God in express words (as it does first the Father and afterwards the Son), becomes to you an occasion of blasphemy and of this excessive wordiness and impiety, we will release you from this inconvenience by a short discussion of things and names, and especially of their use in Holy Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Some things have no existence, but are spoken of; others which do exist are not spoken of; some neither exist nor are spoken of, and some both exist and are spoken of.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you ask me for proof of this?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am ready to give it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: According to Scripture God sleeps and is awake, is angry, walks, has the Cherubim for His Throne.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And yet when did He become liable to passion, and have you ever heard that God has a body?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This then is, though not really fact, a figure of speech.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we have given names according to our own comprehension from our own attributes to those of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His remaining silent apart from us, and as it were not caring for us, for reasons known to Himself, is what we call His sleeping; for our own sleep is such a state of inactivity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And again, His sudden turning to do us good is the waking up; for waking is the dissolution of sleep, as visitation is of turning away.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And when He punishes, we say He is angry; for so it is with us, punishment is the result of anger.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And His working, now here now there, we call walking; for walking is change from one place to another.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His resting among the Holy Hosts, and as it were loving to dwell among them, is His sitting and being enthroned; this, too, from ourselves, for God resteth nowhere as He doth upon the Saints.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His swiftness of moving is called flying, and His watchful care is called His Face, and his giving and bestowing is His hand; and, in a word, every other of the powers or activities of God has depicted for us some other corporeal one.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Again, where do you get your Unbegotten and Unoriginate, those two citadels of your position, or we our Immortal?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Show me these in so many words, or we shall either set them aside, or erase them as not contained in Scripture; and you are slain by your own principle, the names you rely on being overthrown, and therewith the wall of refuge in which you trusted.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is it not evident that they are due to passages which imply them, though the words do not actually occur?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What are these passages?—I am the first, and I am the last, and before Me there was no God, neither shall there be after Me.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For all that depends on that Am makes for my side, for it has neither beginning nor ending.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When you accept this, that nothing is before Him, and that He has not an older Cause, you have implicitly given Him the titles Unbegotten and Unoriginate.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And to say that He has no end of Being is to call Him Immortal and Indestructible.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The first pairs, then, that I referred to are accounted for thus.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But what are the things which neither exist in fact nor are said?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That God is evil; that a sphere is square; that the past is present; that man is not a compound being.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Have you ever known a man of such stupidity as to venture either to think or to assert any such thing?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: It remains to shew what are the things which exist, both in fact and in language.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: God, Man, Angel, Judgment, Vanity (viz., such arguments as yours), and the subversion of faith and emptying of the mystery.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Since, then, there is so much difference in terms and things, why are you such a slave to the letter, and a partisan of the Jewish wisdom, and a follower of syllables at the expense of facts?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if, when you said twice five or twice seven, I concluded from your words that you meant Ten or Fourteen; or if, when you spoke of a rational and mortal animal, that you meant Man, should you think me to be talking nonsense?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Surely not, because I should be merely repeating your own meaning; for words do not belong more to the speaker of them than to him who called them forth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: As, then, in this case, I should have been looking, not so much at the terms used, as at the thoughts they were meant to convey; so neither, if I found something else either not at all or not clearly expressed in the Words of Scripture to be included in the meaning, should I avoid giving it utterance, out of fear of your sophistical trick about terms.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In this way, then, we shall hold our own against the semi-orthodox—among whom I may not count you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For since you deny the Titles of the Son, which are so many and so clear, it is quite evident that even if you learnt a great many more and clearer ones you would not be moved to reverence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But now I will take up the argument again a little way further back, and shew you, though you are so clever, the reason for this entire system of secresy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There have been in the whole period of the duration of the world two conspicuous changes of men’s lives, which are also called two Testaments, or, on account of the wide fame of the matter, two Earthquakes; the one from idols to the Law, the other from the Law to the Gospel.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And we are taught in the Gospel of a third earthquake, namely, from this Earth to that which cannot be shaken or moved.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now the two Testaments are alike in this respect, that the change was not made on a sudden, nor at the first movement of the endeavour.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why not (for this is a point on which we must have information)?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That no violence might be done to us, but that we might be moved by persuasion.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For nothing that is involuntary is durable; like streams or trees which are kept back by force.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But that which is voluntary is more durable and safe.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The former is due to one who uses force, the latter is ours; the one is due to the gentleness of God, the other to a tyrannical authority.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Wherefore God did not think it behoved Him to benefit the unwilling, but to do good to the willing.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And therefore like a Tutor or Physician He partly removes and partly condones ancestral habits, conceding some little of what tended to pleasure, just as medical men do with their patients, that their medicine may be taken, being artfully blended with what is nice.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is no very easy matter to change from those habits which custom and use have made honourable.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For instance, the first cut off the idol, but left the sacrifices; the second, while it destroyed the sacrifices did not forbid circumcision.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Then, when once men had submitted to the curtailment, they also yielded that which had been conceded to them; in the first instance the sacrifices, in the second circumcision; and became instead of Gentiles, Jews, and instead of Jews, Christians, being beguiled into the Gospel by gradual changes.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Paul is a proof of this; for having at one time administered circumcision, and submitted to legal purification, he advanced till he could say, and I, brethren, if I yet preach circumcision, why do I yet suffer persecution?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His former conduct belonged to the temporary dispensation, his latter to maturity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To this I may compare the case of Theology except that it proceeds the reverse way.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in the case by which I have illustrated it the change is made by successive subtractions; whereas here perfection is reached by additions.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the matter stands thus.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The Old Testament proclaimed the Father openly, and the Son more obscurely.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The New manifested the Son, and suggested the Deity of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now the Spirit Himself dwells among us, and supplies us with a clearer demonstration of Himself.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it was not safe, when the Godhead of the Father was not yet acknowledged, plainly to proclaim the Son; nor when that of the Son was not yet received to burden us further (if I may use so bold an expression) with the Holy Ghost; lest perhaps people might, like men loaded with food beyond their strength, and presenting eyes as yet too weak to bear it to the sun’s light, risk the loss even of that which was within the reach of their powers; but that by gradual additions, and, as David says, Goings up, and advances and progress from glory to glory, the Light of the Trinity might shine upon the more illuminated.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For this reason it was, I think, that He gradually came to dwell in the Disciples, measuring Himself out to them according to their capacity to receive Him, at the beginning of the Gospel, after the Passion, after the Ascension, making perfect their powers, being breathed upon them, and appearing in fiery tongues.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And indeed it is by little and little that He is declared by Jesus, as you will learn for yourself if you will read more carefully.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will ask the Father, He says, and He will send you another Comforter, even the spirit of Truth.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This He said that He might not seem to be a rival God, or to make His discourses to them by another authority.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Again, He shall send Him, but it is in My Name.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He leaves out the I will ask, but He keeps the Shall send, then again, I will send,—His own dignity.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Then shall come, the authority of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You see lights breaking upon us, gradually; and the order of Theology, which it is better for us to keep, neither proclaiming things too suddenly, nor yet keeping them hidden to the end.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the former course would be unscientific, the latter atheistical; and the former would be calculated to startle outsiders, the latter to alienate our own people.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will add another point to what I have said; one which may readily have come into the mind of some others, but which I think a fruit of my own thought.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Our Saviour had some things which, He said, could not be borne at that time by His disciples (though they were filled with many teachings), perhaps for the reasons I have mentioned; and therefore they were hidden.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And again He said that all things should be taught us by the Spirit when He should come to dwell amongst us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of these things one, I take it, was the Deity of the Spirit Himself, made clear later on when such knowledge should be seasonable and capable of being received after our Saviour’s restoration, when it would no longer be received with incredulity because of its marvellous character.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For what greater thing than this did either He promise, or the Spirit teach.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If indeed anything is to be considered great and worthy of the Majesty of God, which was either promised or taught.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This, then, is my position with regard to these things, and I hope it may be always my position, and that of whosoever is dear to me; to worship God the Father, God the Son, and God the Holy Ghost, Three Persons, One Godhead, undivided in honour and glory and substance and kingdom, as one of our own inspired philosophers not long departed shewed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let him not see the rising of the Morning Star, as Scripture saith, nor the glory of its brightness, who is otherwise minded, or who follows the temper of the times, at one time being of one mind and of another at another time, and thinking unsoundly in the highest matters.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For if He is not to be worshipped, how can He deify me by Baptism? but if He is to be worshipped, surely He is an Object of adoration, and if an Object of adoration He must be God; the one is linked to the other, a truly golden and saving chain.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And indeed from the Spirit comes our New Birth, and from the New Birth our new creation, and from the new creation our deeper knowledge of the dignity of Him from Whom it is derived.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This, then, is what may be said by one who admits the silence of Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But now the swarm of testimonies shall burst upon you from which the Deity of the Holy Ghost shall be shewn to all who are not excessively stupid, or else altogether enemies to the Spirit, to be most clearly recognized in Scripture.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Look at these facts:—Christ is born; the Spirit is His Forerunner.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is baptized; the Spirit bears witness.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is tempted; the Spirit leads Him up.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He works miracles; the Spirit accompanies them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He ascends; the Spirit takes His place.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What great things are there in the idea of God which are not in His power?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What titles which belong to God are not applied to Him, except only Unbegotten and Begotten?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it was needful that the distinctive properties of the Father and the Son should remain peculiar to Them, lest there should be confusion in the Godhead Which brings all things, even disorder itself, into due arrangement and good order.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Indeed I tremble when I think of the abundance of the titles, and how many Names they outrage who fall foul of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He is called the Spirit of God, the Spirit of Christ, the Mind of Christ, the Spirit of The Lord, and Himself The Lord, the Spirit of Adoption, of Truth, of Liberty; the Spirit of Wisdom, of Understanding, of Counsel, of Might, of Knowledge, of Godliness, of the Fear of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For He is the Maker of all these, filling all with His Essence, containing all things, filling the world in His Essence, yet incapable of being comprehended in His power by the world; good, upright, princely, by nature not by adoption; sanctifying, not sanctified; measuring, not measured; shared, not sharing; filling, not filled; containing, not contained; inherited, glorified, reckoned with the Father and the Son; held out as a threat; the Finger of God; fire like God; to manifest, as I take it, His consubstantiality); the Creator-Spirit, Who by Baptism and by Resurrection creates anew; the Spirit That knoweth all things, That teacheth, That bloweth where and to what extent He listeth; That guideth, talketh, sendeth forth, separateth, is angry or tempted; That revealeth, illumineth, quickeneth, or rather is the very Light and Life; That maketh Temples; That deifieth; That perfecteth so as even to anticipate Baptism, yet after Baptism to be sought as a separate gift; That doeth all things that God doeth; divided into fiery tongues; dividing gifts; making Apostles, Prophets, Evangelists, Pastors, and Teachers; understanding manifold, clear, piercing, undefiled, unhindered, which is the same thing as Most wise and varied in His actions; and making all things clear and plain; and of independent power, unchangeable, Almighty, all-seeing, penetrating all spirits that are intelligent, pure, most subtle (the Angel Hosts I think); and also all prophetic spirits and apostolic in the same manner and not in the same places; for they lived in different places; thus showing that He is uncircumscript.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: They who say and teach these things, and moreover call Him another Paraclete in the sense of another God, who know that blasphemy against Him alone cannot be forgiven, and who branded with such fearful infamy Ananias and Sapphira for having lied to the Holy Ghost, what do you think of these men?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do they proclaim the Spirit God, or something else?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now really, you must be extraordinarily dull and far from the Spirit if you have any doubt about this and need some one to teach you.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So important then, and so vivid are His Names.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Why is it necessary to lay before you the testimony contained in the very words?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And whatever in this case also is said in more lowly fashion, as that He is Given, Sent, Divided; that He is the Gift, the Bounty, the Inspiration, the Promise, the Intercession for us, and, not to go into any further detail, any other expressions of the sort, is to be referred to the First Cause, that it may be shewn from Whom He is, and that men may not in heathen fashion admit Three Principles.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is equally impious to confuse the Persons with the Sabellians, or to divide the Natures with the Arians.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have very carefully considered this matter in my own mind, and have looked at it in every point of view, in order to find some illustration of this most important subject, but I have been unable to discover any thing on earth with which to compare the nature of the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For even if I did happen upon some tiny likeness it escaped me for the most part, and left me down below with my example.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I picture to myself an eye, a fountain, a river, as others have done before, to see if the first might be analogous to the Father, the second to the Son, and the third to the Holy Ghost.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For in these there is no distinction in time, nor are they torn away from their connexion with each other, though they seem to be parted by three personalities.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But I was afraid in the first place that I should present a flow in the Godhead, incapable of standing still; and secondly that by this figure a numerical unity would be introduced.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For the eye and the spring and the river are numerically one, though in different forms.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Again I thought of the sun and a ray and light.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But here again there was a fear lest people should get an idea of composition in the Uncompounded Nature, such as there is in the Sun and the things that are in the Sun.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And in the second place lest we should give Essence to the Father but deny Personality to the Others, and make Them only Powers of God, existing in Him and not Personal.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For neither the ray nor the light is another sun, but they are only effulgences from the Sun, and qualities of His essence.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And lest we should thus, as far as the illustration goes, attribute both Being and Not-being to God, which is even more monstrous.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have also heard that some one has suggested an illustration of the following kind.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: A ray of the Sun flashing upon a wall and trembling with the movement of the moisture which the beam has taken up in mid air, and then, being checked by the hard body, has set up a strange quivering.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it quivers with many rapid movements, and is not one rather than it is many, nor yet many rather than one; because by the swiftness of its union and separating it escapes before the eye can see it.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But it is not possible for me to make use of even this; because it is very evident what gives the ray its motion; but there is nothing prior to God which could set Him in motion; for He is Himself the Cause of all things, and He has no prior Cause.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And secondly because in this case also there is a suggestion of such things as composition, diffusion, and an unsettled and unstable nature…none of which we can suppose in the Godhead.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In a word, there is nothing which presents a standing point to my mind in these illustrations from which to consider the Object which I am trying to represent to myself, unless one may indulgently accept one point of the image while rejecting the rest.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Finally, then, it seems best to me to let the images and the shadows go, as being deceitful and very far short of the truth; and clinging myself to the more reverent conception, and resting upon few words, using the guidance of the Holy Ghost, keeping to the end as my genuine comrade and companion the enlightenment which I have received from Him, and passing through this world to persuade all others also to the best of my power to worship Father, Son, and Holy Ghost, the One Godhead and Power.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To Him belongs all glory and honour and might for ever and ever.
